revision.text: #redirect[[en:Ubusuna_Hachiman_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

[[ファイル:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG |thumb |[[拝殿]]]] [[ファイル:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG |thumb |拝殿より見た楠]] '''産土八幡神社'''（うぶすなはちまんじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]]の[[湯河原町]]にある神社。祭神は[[応神天皇|誉田別命（応神天皇）]]<!--読みがなもこの出典--><ref name="kanagawa-jinja">{{Cite web |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=産土八幡神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>。[[五所神社 (湯河原町)|五所神社]]の[[兼務神社]]<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=城堀産土八幡神社例大祭（兼務社） |publisher=五所神社 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>。
[[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年）創立と伝えられるがその他の古い出来事については明らかでない<ref name="kanagawa-jinja" /><ref name="asgr_195">{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=足柄下郡神社誌 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.}} |page=195 }}</ref> 。境内に二つある鳥居のうち、古い方には[[貞享]]4年[[丁卯]]9月とある<ref>前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=196}}。</ref>。[[1873年]]7月30日に[[村社]]となる<ref name="asgr_195" />。1931年7月に境内の拡張があり、これにあわせて社殿は草葺であったものを亜鉛版葺とした<ref name="asgr_200">前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=200}}。</ref>。また、同年11年13日に神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定された<ref name="asgr_195" />。1960年9月には社殿を瓦葺とした<ref name="asgr_200" />。1967年5月には新しい鳥居が追加された<ref>前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=198}}。</ref>。
1930年発行の『湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて』には「境内は数百年を経た老樹鬱蒼として神さび、えもいはれない幽趣ある地である。」と紹介されているが<ref>{{Citation |和書 |year=1930 |title=湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて |publisher=足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会|1930}} |page=59 |url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1095505}}</ref>、この「老樹」のひとつは現存している。神社の石段の右手にある推定年齢600年の木がそれで、「産土八幡神社の[[クスノキ|楠]]」として1997年5月1日に湯河原町指定の天然記念物とされた<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=湯河原町 天然記念物 |publisher=湯河原町役場社会教育課 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>。
== アクセス == [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]]の[[湯河原駅]]より徒歩4分<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=産土八幡神社 神奈川県足柄下郡湯河原町城堀/寺院、神社の詳細情報 - Yahoo!ロコ |publisher=[[Yahoo! JAPAN]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>。

1930年発行の『湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて』には「境内は数百年を経た老樹鬱蒼として神さび、えもいはれない幽趣ある地である。」と紹介されているが<ref>{{Citation |和書 |year=1930 |title=湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて |publisher=足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会|1930}} |page=59 |url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1095505}}</ref>、この「老樹」のひとつは現存している。神社の石段の右手にある推定年齢600年の木がそれで、「産土八幡神社の[[クスノキ|楠]]」として1997年5月1日に湯河原町指定の天然記念物とされた<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=湯河原町 天然記念物 |publisher=湯河原町役場社会教育課 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>。
== アクセス == [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]]の[[湯河原駅]]より徒歩4分<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=産土八幡神社 神奈川県足柄下郡湯河原町城堀/寺院、神社の詳細情報 - Yahoo!ロコ |publisher=[[Yahoo! JAPAN]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>。

revision.comment: プロジェクト神道に合わせた祭神の振り仮名の追加/歴史の加筆/西暦と年号の併記になるよう修正
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 産土八幡神社 |画像 = [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
拝殿正面 |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 08 | 緯度秒 = 49.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 06 | 経度秒 = 18.4 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kanagawa | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|村社]] |創建 = [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年） |本殿 = |別名 = 立正八幡宮（旧称） |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''産土八幡神社'''（うぶすなはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]にある神社。旧社格は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 *[[応神天皇|誉田別命（応神天皇）]](ホムダワケ){{Cite web |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=産土八幡神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 歴史 == [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年）創立と伝えられるがその他の古い出来事については明らかでない{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=足柄下郡神社誌 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.}} |page=195 }}。境内に二つある鳥居のうち、古い方には[[貞享]]4年（1687年）[[丁卯]]9月とある前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=196}}。。また、棟札によって1852年（嘉永5年） には改築されていることがわかり、棟札の墨書から立正八幡宮と呼ばれていたことがわかる[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html 年歴表 - 城堀区会ホームページ ]2018年11月19日　閲覧。[[1873年]]（明治6年）7月30日に[[村社]]となる。1931年（昭和6年）7月に境内の拡張があり、これにあわせて社殿は草葺であったものを亜鉛版葺とした前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=200}}。。また、同年11月13日に神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定された。1960年（昭和35年）9月には社殿を瓦葺とした。1967年（昭和42年）5月には新しい鳥居が追加された前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=198}}。。現在は[[五所神社 (湯河原町)|五所神社]]の[[兼務神社]]となっている{{Cite web |url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=城堀産土八幡神社例大祭（兼務社） |publisher=五所神社 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG |thumb |拝殿より見た楠]] 1930年発行の『湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて』には「境内は数百年を経た老樹鬱蒼として神さび、えもいはれない幽趣ある地である。」と紹介されており{{Citation |和書 |year=1930 |title=湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて |publisher=足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会|1930}} |page=59 |url=http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1095505}}、この「老樹」のひとつが神社の石段の右手にある推定年齢600年の木として現在も残っており、「産土八幡神社の[[クスノキ|楠]]」として1997年（平成9年）5月1日に湯河原町指定の天然記念物に指定された{{Cite web |url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=湯河原町 天然記念物 |publisher=湯河原町役場社会教育課 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]][[湯河原駅]]下車後、徒歩4分{{Cite web |url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=産土八幡神社 神奈川県足柄下郡湯河原町城堀/寺院、神社の詳細情報 - Yahoo!ロコ |publisher=[[Yahoo! JAPAN]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 産土八幡神社 - 神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=神奈川県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うふすなはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:湯河原町]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

revision.comment: 外部リンクの修正 http:// -> https:// (dl.ndl.go.jp) ([[Wikipedia:Bot|Bot]]による編集)
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 産土八幡神社 |画像 = [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
拝殿正面 |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 08 | 緯度秒 = 49.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 06 | 経度秒 = 18.4 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kanagawa | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|村社]] |創建 = [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年） |本殿 = |別名 = 立正八幡宮（旧称） |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''産土八幡神社'''（うぶすなはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]にある神社。旧社格は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 *[[応神天皇|誉田別命（応神天皇）]](ホムダワケ){{Cite web |url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=産土八幡神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 歴史 == [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年）創立と伝えられるがその他の古い出来事については明らかでない{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=足柄下郡神社誌 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.}} |page=195 }}。境内に二つある鳥居のうち、古い方には[[貞享]]4年（1687年）[[丁卯]]9月とある前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=196}}。。また、棟札によって1852年（嘉永5年） には改築されていることがわかり、棟札の墨書から立正八幡宮と呼ばれていたことがわかる[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html 年歴表 - 城堀区会ホームページ ]2018年11月19日　閲覧。[[1873年]]（明治6年）7月30日に[[村社]]となる。1931年（昭和6年）7月に境内の拡張があり、これにあわせて社殿は草葺であったものを亜鉛版葺とした前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=200}}。。また、同年11月13日に神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定された。1960年（昭和35年）9月には社殿を瓦葺とした。1967年（昭和42年）5月には新しい鳥居が追加された前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=198}}。。現在は[[五所神社 (湯河原町)|五所神社]]の[[兼務神社]]となっている{{Cite web |url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=城堀産土八幡神社例大祭（兼務社） |publisher=五所神社 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG |thumb |拝殿より見た楠]] 1930年発行の『湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて』には「境内は数百年を経た老樹鬱蒼として神さび、えもいはれない幽趣ある地である。」と紹介されており{{Citation |和書 |year=1930 |title=湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて |publisher=足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会|1930}} |page=59 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1095505}}、この「老樹」のひとつが神社の石段の右手にある推定年齢600年の木として現在も残っており、「産土八幡神社の[[クスノキ|楠]]」として1997年（平成9年）5月1日に湯河原町指定の天然記念物に指定された{{Cite web |url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=湯河原町 天然記念物 |publisher=湯河原町役場社会教育課 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]][[湯河原駅]]下車後、徒歩4分{{Cite web |url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=産土八幡神社 神奈川県足柄下郡湯河原町城堀/寺院、神社の詳細情報 - Yahoo!ロコ |publisher=[[Yahoo! JAPAN]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 産土八幡神社 - 神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=神奈川県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うふすなはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:湯河原町]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

{{神社 |名称 = 産土八幡神社 |画像 = [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
拝殿正面 |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 08 | 緯度秒 = 49.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 06 | 経度秒 = 18.4 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kanagawa | ラベル位置 = left |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|村社]] |創建 = [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年） |本殿 = |別名 = 立正八幡宮（旧称） |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''産土八幡神社'''（うぶすなはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]にある神社。旧社格は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; JAPAN]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 産土八幡神社 - 神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=神奈川県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うふすなはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

拝殿正面
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 08 | 緯度秒 = 49.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 06 | 経度秒 = 18.4 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kanagawa | ラベル位置 = right |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|村社]] |創建 = [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年） |本殿 = |別名 = 立正八幡宮（旧称） |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''産土八幡神社'''（うぶすなはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]にある神社。旧社格は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 *[[応神天皇|誉田別命（応神天皇）]](ホムダワケ){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=産土八幡神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 歴史 == [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年）創立と伝えられるがその他の古い出来事については明らかでない{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=足柄下郡神社誌 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.}} |page=195 }}。境内に二つある鳥居のうち、古い方には[[貞享]]4年（1687年）[[丁卯]]9月とある前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=196}}。。また、棟札によって1852年（嘉永5年） には改築されていることがわかり、棟札の墨書から立正八幡宮と呼ばれていたことがわかる[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html 年歴表 - 城堀区会ホームページ ]2018年11月19日　閲覧。[[1873年]]（明治6年）7月30日に[[村社]]となる。1931年（昭和6年）7月に境内の拡張があり、これにあわせて社殿は草葺であったものを亜鉛版葺とした前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=200}}。。また、同年11月13日に神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定された。1960年（昭和35年）9月には社殿を瓦葺とした。1967年（昭和42年）5月には新しい鳥居が追加された前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=198}}。。現在は[[五所神社 (湯河原町)|五所神社]]の[[兼務神社]]となっている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=城堀産土八幡神社例大祭（兼務社） |publisher=五所神社 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG |thumb |拝殿より見た楠]] 1930年発行の『湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて』には「境内は数百年を経た老樹鬱蒼として神さび、えもいはれない幽趣ある地である。」と紹介されており{{Citation |和書 |year=1930 |title=湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて |publisher=足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会|1930}} |page=59 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1095505}}、この「老樹」のひとつが神社の石段の右手にある推定年齢600年の木として現在も残っており、「産土八幡神社の[[クスノキ|楠]]」として1997年（平成9年）5月1日に湯河原町指定の天然記念物に指定された{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=湯河原町 天然記念物 |publisher=湯河原町役場社会教育課 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]][[湯河原駅]]下車後、徒歩4分{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=産土八幡神社 神奈川県足柄下郡湯河原町城堀/寺院、神社の詳細情報 - Yahoo!ロコ |publisher=[[Yahoo! JAPAN]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 産土八幡神社 - 神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=神奈川県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うふすなはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

revision.text:
'''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine'''
[[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{Shrine |Name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |Image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |Location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |Deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate Name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake no Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|五所神社}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{Shrine |Name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |Image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |Location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |Deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate Name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake no Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{Shrine |Name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |Image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |Location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |Deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate Name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake no Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |Deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake no Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |Deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]

One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]

{{神社 |名称 = 産土八幡神社 |画像 = [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
拝殿正面
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 08 | 緯度秒 = 49.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 06 | 経度秒 = 18.4 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kanagawa | ラベル位置 = right |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|村社]] |創建 = [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年） |本殿 = |別名 = 立正八幡宮（旧称） |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''産土八幡神社'''（うぶすなはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]にある神社。旧社格は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 *[[応神天皇|誉田別命（応神天皇）]](ホムダワケ){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=産土八幡神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 歴史 == [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年）創立と伝えられるがその他の古い出来事については明らかでない{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=足柄下郡神社誌 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.}} |page=195 }}。境内に二つある鳥居のうち、古い方には[[貞享]]4年（1687年）[[丁卯]]9月とある前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=196}}。。また、棟札によって1852年（嘉永5年） には改築されていることがわかり、棟札の墨書から立正八幡宮と呼ばれていたことがわかる[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html 年歴表 - 城堀区会ホームページ ]2018年11月19日　閲覧。[[1873年]]（明治6年）7月30日に[[村社]]となる。1931年（昭和6年）7月に境内の拡張があり、これにあわせて社殿は草葺であったものを亜鉛版葺とした前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=200}}。。また、同年11月13日に神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定された。1960年（昭和35年）9月には社殿を瓦葺とした。1967年（昭和42年）5月には新しい鳥居が追加された前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=198}}。。現在は[[五所神社 (湯河原町)|五所神社]]の[[兼務神社]]となっている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=城堀産土八幡神社例大祭（兼務社） |publisher=五所神社 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG |thumb |拝殿より見た楠]] 1930年発行の『湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて』には「境内は数百年を経た老樹鬱蒼として神さび、えもいはれない幽趣ある地である。」と紹介されており{{Citation |和書 |year=1930 |title=湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて |publisher=足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会|1930}} |page=59 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1095505}}、この「老樹」のひとつが神社の石段の右手にある推定年齢600年の木として現在も残っており、「産土八幡神社の[[クスノキ|楠]]」として1997年（平成9年）5月1日に湯河原町指定の天然記念物に指定された{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=湯河原町 天然記念物 |publisher=湯河原町役場社会教育課 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]][[湯河原駅]]下車後、徒歩4分。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 産土八幡神社 - 神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=神奈川県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うふすなはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! {{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! {{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine {{神社 |名称 = 産土八幡神社 |画像 = [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
拝殿正面
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 08 | 緯度秒 = 49.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 06 | 経度秒 = 18.4 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kanagawa | ラベル位置 = right |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|村社]] |創建 = [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年） |本殿 = |別名 = 立正八幡宮（旧称） |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''産土八幡神社'''（うぶすなはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]にある神社。旧社格は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 *[[応神天皇|誉田別命（応神天皇）]](ホムダワケ){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=産土八幡神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 歴史 == [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年）創立と伝えられるがその他の古い出来事については明らかでない{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=足柄下郡神社誌 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.}} |page=195 }}。境内に二つある[[鳥居]]のうち、古い方には[[貞享]]4年（1687年）[[丁卯]]9月とある前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=196}}。。また、棟札によって1852年（嘉永5年） には改築されていることがわかり、棟札の墨書から立正八幡宮と呼ばれていたことがわかる[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html 年歴表 - 城堀区会ホームページ ]2018年11月19日　閲覧。[[1873年]]（明治6年）7月30日に[[村社]]となる。1931年（昭和6年）7月に境内の拡張があり、これにあわせて社殿は草葺であったものを亜鉛版葺とした前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=200}}。。また、同年11月13日に神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定された。1960年（昭和35年）9月には社殿を瓦葺とした。1967年（昭和42年）5月には新しい鳥居が追加された前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=198}}。。現在は[[五所神社 (湯河原町)|五所神社]]の[[兼務神社]]となっている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=城堀産土八幡神社例大祭（兼務社） |publisher=五所神社 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG |thumb |拝殿より見た楠]] 1930年発行の『湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて』には「境内は数百年を経た老樹鬱蒼として神さび、えもいはれない幽趣ある地である。」と紹介されており{{Citation |和書 |year=1930 |title=湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて |publisher=足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会|1930}} |page=59 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1095505}}、この「老樹」のひとつが神社の石段の右手にある推定年齢600年の木として現在も残っており、「産土八幡神社の[[クスノキ|楠]]」として1997年（平成9年）5月1日に湯河原町指定の天然記念物に指定された{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=湯河原町 天然記念物 |publisher=湯河原町役場社会教育課 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]][[湯河原駅]]下車後、徒歩4分。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 産土八幡神社 - 神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=神奈川県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うふすなはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine
