{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} [[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]]

{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - {{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - {{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - {{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|12=draft}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|12=draft}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|12=draft|12=draft}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|12=draft|12=draft}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|12=draft|12=draft}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]]

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:産土八幡神社 up to rev 104024627
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 産土八幡神社 |画像 = [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
拝殿正面
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 08 | 緯度秒 = 49.4 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 06 | 経度秒 = 18.4 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kanagawa | ラベル位置 = right |祭神 = [[応神天皇|誉田別命]] |社格 = 旧[[近代社格制度|村社]] |創建 = [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年） |本殿 = |別名 = 立正八幡宮（旧称） |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''産土八幡神社'''（うぶすなはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]にある神社。旧社格は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主祭神 *[[応神天皇|誉田別命（応神天皇）]](ホムダワケ){{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=産土八幡神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 歴史 == [[1108年]]（[[天仁]]元年）創立と伝えられるがその他の古い出来事については明らかでない{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=足柄下郡神社誌 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.}} |page=195 }}。境内に二つある[[鳥居]]のうち、古い方には[[貞享]]4年（1687年）[[丁卯]]9月とある前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=196}}。。また、棟札によって1852年（嘉永5年） には改築されていることがわかり、棟札の墨書から立正八幡宮と呼ばれていたことがわかる[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html 年歴表 - 城堀区会ホームページ ]2018年11月19日　閲覧。[[1873年]]（明治6年）7月30日に[[村社]]となる。1931年（昭和6年）7月に境内の拡張があり、これにあわせて社殿は草葺であったものを亜鉛版葺とした前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=200}}。。また、同年11月13日に神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定された。1960年（昭和35年）9月には社殿を瓦葺とした。1967年（昭和42年）5月には新しい鳥居が追加された前掲{{harv|足柄下郡神社誌|n.d.|p=198}}。。現在は[[五所神社 (湯河原町)|五所神社]]の[[兼務神社]]となっている{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=城堀産土八幡神社例大祭（兼務社） |publisher=五所神社 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG |thumb |拝殿より見た楠]] 1930年発行の『湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて』には「境内は数百年を経た老樹鬱蒼として神さび、えもいはれない幽趣ある地である。」と紹介されており{{Citation |和書 |year=1930 |title=湘南五郷の史蹟を訪ねて |publisher=足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会 |ref = {{SfnRef|足柄下郡教育会第五部研究会|1930}} |page=59 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1095505}}、この「老樹」のひとつが神社の石段の右手にある推定年齢600年の木として現在も残っており、「産土八幡神社の[[クスノキ|楠]]」として1997年（平成9年）5月1日に湯河原町指定の天然記念物に指定された{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=湯河原町 天然記念物 |publisher=湯河原町役場社会教育課 |accessdate=2017-06-11}}。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[湯河原町]][[城堀]]216 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]][[湯河原駅]]下車後、徒歩4分。 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 産土八幡神社 - 神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=神奈川県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うふすなはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[産土八幡神社]] to [[Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:

神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=神奈川県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うふすなはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} {{citation needed|Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]]
