One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennin|qq=}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|ja|湯河原町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugawara|qq=}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashigarashimo District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ({{ill|Tennin|ja|天仁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennin|qq=}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō|qq=}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaei|qq=}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|ja|湯河原駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugawara Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]]

The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]]

{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yugawara]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Buildings and structures in Yugawara
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ubusuna Shrines]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原町の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:湯河原の Municipal History]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:湯河原の Municipal History]] into [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:湯河原町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30935830]] Wikidata name: Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[sn:busuna Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}}

In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}}

[[ja:産土八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30935830]] Wikidata name: Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}}

[[ja:産土八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30935830]] Wikidata name: Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}}

Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}}

revision.text:
[[ja:産土八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30935830]] Wikidata name: Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}}
