{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:産土八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30935830]] Wikidata name: Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[ja:産土八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30935830]] Wikidata name: Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:産土八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30935830]] Wikidata name: Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}}

In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30935830]] Wikidata name: Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:産土八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q30935830]] Wikidata name: Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]]
Front view of the worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa]], [[Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation=[[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Shirohori, [[:en:Yugawara|Yugawara]], [[:en:Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|Ashigarashimo District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in [[:en:1108|1108]] ([[:en:Tennin (era)|Tennin]] 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ([[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in [[:en:1960|1960]] (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from [[:en:Yugawara Station|Yugawara Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|JR Tōkaidō Main Line]].{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|1108|en|1108|WD=Q19514|lt=1108}} ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|WD=Q1193599|lt=Tennin}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|en|Yugawara Station|WD=Q841936|lt=Yugawara Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=JR Tōkaidō Main Line}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Shōwa 35). {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|8|49.4|N|139|6|18.4|E}}}} |location={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa}}, [[Ashigarashimo District]], {{ill|Yugawara|en|Yugawara|WD=Q532346|lt=Yugawara}}, Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation={{ill|1108|en|1108|WD=Q19514|lt=1108}} ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|WD=Q1193599|lt=Tennin}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) }} '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|en|Yugawara|WD=Q532346|lt=Yugawara}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == deity == ; Main deity * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|1108|en|1108|WD=Q19514|lt=1108}} ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|WD=Q1193599|lt=Tennin}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }} One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 4 (1687).Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}. A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018. In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}. In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社). The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Shōwa 35). In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}. Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara Town)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}} == location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan. ; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|en|Yugawara Station|WD=Q841936|lt=Yugawara Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=JR Tōkaidō Main Line}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}} ==Notelist== == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Agency] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|産土八幡神社|version=104024627|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:産土八幡神社]] [[ja:%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{wikidata link|Q30935830}} [[Category:Buildings in Yugawara Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Yugawara]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

'''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|en|Yugawara|WD=Q532346|lt=Yugawara}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== deity == ; Main deity * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="kanagawa-jinja">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|1108|en|1108|WD=Q19514|lt=1108}} ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|WD=Q1193599|lt=Tennin}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.<ref name="kanagawa-jinja" /><ref name="asgr_195">{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }}</ref> One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 4 (1687).<ref>Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}.</ref> A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.<ref>[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018.</ref>
In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="asgr_195" /> In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.<ref name="asgr_200">Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}.</ref> In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社).<ref name="asgr_195" />
The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Shōwa 35).<ref name="asgr_200" /> In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.<ref>Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}.</ref> Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|五所神社 (湯河原町)|12=|13=|ja_comment=|comment=|WD=Q30935875|lt=Gosho Shrine}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>

; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|en|Yugawara Station|WD=Q841936|lt=Yugawara Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=JR Tōkaidō Main Line}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! '''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|en|Yugawara|WD=Q532346|lt=Yugawara}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== deity == ; Main deity * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="kanagawa-jinja">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|1108|en|1108|WD=Q19514|lt=1108}} ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|WD=Q1193599|lt=Tennin}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.<ref name="kanagawa-jinja" /><ref name="asgr_195">{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }}</ref> One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 4 (1687).<ref>Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}.</ref> A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.<ref>[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018.</ref>
In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="asgr_195" /> In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.<ref name="asgr_200">Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}.</ref> In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社).<ref name="asgr_195" />
The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Shōwa 35).<ref name="asgr_200" /> In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.<ref>Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}.</ref> Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|五所神社 (湯河原町)|12=|13=|ja_comment=|comment=|WD=Q30935875|lt=Gosho Shrine}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan.
; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|en|Yugawara Station|WD=Q841936|lt=Yugawara Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=JR Tōkaidō Main Line}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]] |location={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa}}, [[Ashigarashimo District]], {{ill|Yugawara|en|Yugawara|WD=Q532346|lt=Yugawara}}, Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation={{ill|1108|en|1108|WD=Q19514|lt=1108}} ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|WD=Q1193599|lt=Tennin}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) |coordinates={{coord|35.147055555556|139.10511111111}}|map_type=Japan}}
'''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|en|Yugawara|WD=Q532346|lt=Yugawara}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== deity == ; Main deity * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="kanagawa-jinja">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|1108|en|1108|WD=Q19514|lt=1108}} ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|WD=Q1193599|lt=Tennin}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.<ref name="kanagawa-jinja" /><ref name="asgr_195">{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }}</ref> One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} 4 (1687).<ref>Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}.</ref> A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.<ref>[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018.</ref>
In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="asgr_195" /> In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.<ref name="asgr_200">Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}.</ref> In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社).<ref name="asgr_195" />
The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960}} (Shōwa 35).<ref name="asgr_200" /> In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.<ref>Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}.</ref> Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|五所神社 (湯河原町)|12=|13=|ja_comment=|comment=|WD=Q30935875|lt=Gosho Shrine}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan.
; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|en|Yugawara Station|WD=Q841936|lt=Yugawara Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=JR Tōkaidō Main Line}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:産土八幡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:産土八幡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:産土八幡神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |image=[[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_haiden.JPG|250px]] |location={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, [[Ashigarashimo District]], {{ill|Yugawara|en|Yugawara|WD=Q532346|lt=Yugawara|1=Yugawara}}, Shirohori 216 |Latitude degrees=35 |Latitude minutes=08 |Latitude seconds=49.4 |North/South=N |Longitude degrees=139 |Longitude minutes=06 |Longitude seconds=18.4 |East/West=E |Map Country Code=JP |Map=Japan Kanagawa |Label Position=right |deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |Status=Former [[village shrine|mura-sha]] |Foundation={{ill|1108|en|1108|WD=Q19514|lt=1108|1=1108}} ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|WD=Q1193599|lt=Tennin|1=Tennin (era)}} 1) |Alternate name=Risshō Hachiman Shrine (former name) |coordinates={{coord|35.147055555556|139.10511111111}}|map_type=Japan}}
'''Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine''' (産土八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shirohori, {{ill|Yugawara|en|Yugawara|WD=Q532346|lt=Yugawara|1=Yugawara}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine was formerly designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== deity == ; Main deity * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (誉田別命), also known as Emperor Ōjin.<ref name="kanagawa-jinja">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1013&cd=1210250&scd=&npg=1 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Shrine Agency |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== History == The shrine is said to have been established in {{ill|1108|en|1108|WD=Q19514|lt=1108|1=1108}} ({{ill|Tennin (era)|en|Tennin (era)|WD=Q1193599|lt=Tennin|1=Tennin (era)}} 1), though the details of earlier events remain unclear.<ref name="kanagawa-jinja" /><ref name="asgr_195">{{Citation |和書 |year=n.d. |title=Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records |ref = {{SfnRef|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.}} |page=195 }}</ref> One of the two torii gates in the precincts is inscribed with the date {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} 4 (1687).<ref>Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=196}}.</ref> A ridge tag indicates that the shrine was rebuilt in 1852 ({{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} 5) and identifies its earlier name as Risshō Hachiman Shrine.<ref>[http://shirohori.web.fc2.com/nenreki.html Chronology - Shirohori District Homepage] Accessed November 19, 2018.</ref>
In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="asgr_195" /> In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the precincts were expanded, and the shrine's thatched roof was replaced with galvanized sheet roofing.<ref name="asgr_200">Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=200}}.</ref> In November of the same year, the shrine was designated to receive government offerings (神饌幣帛料供進神社).<ref name="asgr_195" />
The roof was upgraded to tiled roofing in {{ill|1960|en|1960|WD=Q3754|lt=1960|1=1960}} (Shōwa 35).<ref name="asgr_200" /> In 1967 (Shōwa 42), a new torii gate was erected.<ref>Previously cited {{harv|Ashigarashimo District Shrine Records|n.d.|p=198}}.</ref> Currently, the shrine is managed as a branch of the {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Yugawara)|ja|五所神社 (湯河原町)|12=|13=|ja_comment=|comment=|WD=Q30935875|lt=Gosho Shrine|1=Gosho Shrine}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://goshojinjya.com/activity/%E5%9F%8E%E5%A0%80%E7%94%A3%E5%9C%9F%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E4%BE%8B%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%AD%EF%BC%88%E5%85%BC%E5%8B%99%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%89-4/ |title=Shirohori Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine festival |publisher=Gosho Shrine |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== Grounds == [[File:ubusunahachimanjinja_kusunoki1.JPG|thumb|View of the sacred camphor tree from the worship hall]] The 1930 publication "Visiting the Historic Sites of Shōnan's Five Districts" describes the precincts as "a sacred site with venerable old trees that exude an ineffable tranquility." One such ancient tree, estimated to be 600 years old, still stands to the right of the shrine's stone steps. This tree, known as the "Camphor Tree of Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine," was designated a natural monument of Yugawara on May 1, 1997.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yugawara.kanagawa.jp/kyoiku/rekishibunnka/natural-monument.html#unit-4706 |title=Natural Monuments of Yugawara |publisher=Yugawara Town Board of Education |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
== location == ; Address * Shirohori 216, Yugawara Town, Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan.
; Access * Nearest station: 4-minute walk from {{ill|Yugawara Station|en|Yugawara Station|WD=Q841936|lt=Yugawara Station|1=Yugawara Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=JR Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://loco.yahoo.co.jp/place/g-XQMoMYf1PH6 |title=Ubusuna Hachiman Shrine - Details |publisher=[[Yahoo! Japan]] |accessdate=2017-06-11}}</ref>
